commands.review_summary:
The PR adds launcher and entry fast paths for precomputed `doctor`, `gateway`, `models`, and `plugins` help, and switches startup RSS hook imports to file URL specifiers in benchmark tooling.

PR surface: Source +67, Tests +158, Other +29. Total +254 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a performance PR rather than a bug report. Current main source shows generated subcommand help exists, but the outer launcher still falls through to `dist/entry.js` for these direct subcommand help invocations.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair job is needed; there is no discrete patch defect for automation and the remaining action is ordinary maintainer/CI review of a draft performance PR.

target.title: Speed up precomputed command help startup
target.body:
## Summary
- add strict launcher fast paths for precomputed `doctor`, `gateway`, `models`, and `plugins` help
- keep container routing in control for those new help paths
- fix Windows `node --import` RSS hook paths in startup benchmark tooling

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write openclaw.mjs test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts src/entry.ts src/entry.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.test.ts test/scripts/bench-cli-startup.test.ts test/scripts/check-cli-startup-memory.test.ts`
- focused launcher e2e for precomputed subcommand help and container fallthrough
- `git diff --check`
- `codex-review --uncommitted` clean after fixing accepted findings
- final startup benchmark: `doctor --help` 50.5 ms avg, `gateway --help` 59.1 ms, `models --help` 61.5 ms, `plugins --help` 55.5 ms; direct `dist/entry.js` fallback was 217-282 ms with about 76-77 MB RSS vs about 46 MB RSS on the fast path
target.author: yyzquwu
